data.title: Lion Group to Change ADS Ratio
data.description:
Lion Group said Friday it plans to change the ratio of its American depositary shares to its Class A shares.

The new ratio will be one ADS to 2,500 Class A shares from the current ADS ratio of one ADS to 50 Class A shares, Lion said. It expects the change to be effective March 26.

The ratio change will have the same effect as a 1-for-50 reverse ADS split and is intended to help the company regain compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement, it said.

data.title: US judge blocks Trump administration from deporting immigration activist
data.description:
A U.S. judge on Friday temporarily barred the Trump administration from deporting an immigration activist whose detention in Colorado this week outraged state Democrats and immigration rights advocates.

Jeanette Vizguerra, 53, a Mexican national who made headlines during President Donald Trump's first term by moving into a church to avoid deportation, cannot be moved out of Colorado without further court action, U.S. District Judge Nina Wang ruled.

The judge has scheduled a hearing on March 28.

Vizguerra was arrested in Denver on Monday, according to a post on X from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. She has filed petitions in both federal district court and a U.S. appeals court challenging her detention order as invalid.

The Trump administration has implemented a wide-ranging immigration crackdown since he took office in January, following a campaign in which he vowed to institute mass deportations and sharply reduce illegal crossings at the U.S.-Mexico border.

Vizguerra's arrest sparked a protest outside the ICE facility where she is being held. Several Colorado Democrats have criticized her detention, noting that she has several children who are U.S. citizens and has never been accused of a violent crime.

"This is Soviet-style political persecution of political dissidents under the guise of immigration enforcement," Denver Mayor Mike Johnston said following the arrest. "This is a mom of American citizens who works at Target and has started a community non-profit."

ICE did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Friday's court order.

Vizguerra first entered the U.S. illegally in 1997, according to a 2019 lawsuit she filed against ICE. She has been fighting various deportation efforts since 2009, when she was found with a fraudulent Social Security card during a traffic stop and pleaded guilty to a misdemeanor.

She drew national attention when she began living in a church in 2017 to prevent the first Trump administration from deporting her and later received a one-year reprieve from the Biden administration in 2021.

data.title: Rhinebeck Bancorp President/CEO Michael Quinn to Retire
data.description:
Rhinebeck Bancorp late Friday said Michael J. Quinn will retire as chief executive officer and president, after 40 years at the bank, including the last 20 years as CEO.

No successor was immediately named. The company has retained an executive search firm to assist with identifying a new chief executive, with plans to evaluate both internal and external candidates.

Quinn is expected to remain at Rhinebeck until the end of 2025 or when a successor is named, the bank said.

data.title: S&P 500 Rises on Week as Energy, Financials Lead Gainers
data.description:
The Standard and Poor's 500 index rose 0.5% for its first weekly gain since mid-February as markets responded to the Federal Reserve's decision to keep interest rates unchanged.

The S&P 500 ended Friday's session at 5,667.56. The index is still in the red for the month, down 4.8% for March so far, and has dropped 3.6% from where it ended 2024.

Investors have been concerned about uncertainty in monetary policy and the impact of tariffs on inflation in the near term, contributing to the declines in recent weeks.

On Wednesday, they received some clarity as the Federal Reserve's Federal Open Market Committee held the current range of the federal funds rate at 4.25% to 4.50% and said it still sees two rate cuts in each of the next two years and one in 2027. Still, the FOMC increased its inflation forecasts for 2025 and lowered its growth forecast due to uncertainty.

Following the decision, President Donald Trump renewed calls on the Federal Reserve to reduce rates and said the Fed would be much better off cutting rates as US tariffs start to ease.

New York Federal Reserve President John Williams said while monetary policy faces uncertainty, the current restrictive approach is suitable given a robust job market and inflation exceeding the 2% target, allowing for future adjustments.

US existing home sales rebounded unexpectedly in February as more inventory helped bring buyers to the market, according to National Association of Realtors data. NAR Chief Economist Lawrence Yun said that home buyers are slowly entering the market while mortgage rates remain stable. More inventory and choice availability are releasing pent-up housing demand, Yun said.

The energy and financial sectors of the S&P 500 were the top gainers this week and were up 3.2% and 1.9%, respectively. Consumer staples had the largest percentage drop, sliding 0.26%, followed by a 0.25% decline in materials, a 0.21% loss in utilities and a 0.1% decrease in communication services.

Hess (HES) was one of the top performers in the energy sector, gaining over 5.7%. Chevron (CVX) acquired 15.4 million shares of Hess between January and March, representing about 4.99% of all outstanding shares as of Jan. 31.

In financials, Apollo Global Management (APO) was up 6.8%. The private equity firm agreed to acquire a majority stake in OEG Energy Group from Oaktree Capital Management and other investors. Additionally, it agreed to purchase a 25% non-controlling stake in BP's (BP) 12% interest in the pipeline carrying natural gas from Azerbaijan through Turkey.

Nucor (NUE) was hit hardest in the materials sector, falling more than 7.6%. The company said it expects Q1 adjusted earnings in the range of $0.50 and $0.60 per diluted share. Analysts polled by FactSet expected $1.06 at the time.

Next week, earnings reports are expected from companies including Cintas (CTAS), Paychex (PAYX) and Lululemon Athletica (LULU).

Economic data will include February new home sales, pending home sales, durable goods orders and the advanced US trade balance.

data.title: Prosper Gold Details Financing To Fund Exploration Activities at the Cyprus Project
data.description:
Prosper Gold Corp. after trade Friday announced a non-brokered private placement financing of common shares. A statement noted the financing will be completed in one or more tranches and consist of up to five million flow through shares at a price of $0.10 per FT Share for gross proceeds to the company of up to $500,000.

Prosper Gold said it expects to use the net proceeds from the financing to fund exploration activities at the company's Cyprus Project. Completion of the financing is subject to customary closing conditions including the approval of the TSX Venture Exchange

data.title: Consolidating while awaiting tariff clarity
data.description:
TRADING DAY

Late push lifts Nasdaq to first gain in 5 weeks

Many of the world's major central banks sent a strong message this week that the uncertainty caused by U.S. President Donald Trump's trade wars is weighing on growth, stoking inflation, and dramatically reducing visibility on the interest rate outlook.

It's a highly unpredictable and nervy environment for investors to navigate, as reflected by the lack of clear direction across world markets this week.

The MSCI World equity index snapped a four-week losing streak for a rise of 0.7%, the S&P 500 rose 0.5%, the Nasdaq eked out a gain of 0.17% - just avoiding its worst run since the 2022 bear market - while European stocks gained more than 1% for their best week in five weeks.

U.S. high yield credit spreads tightened from the previous week's six-month wides but gold rose, while Treasury yields edged lower yet the dollar crept higher.

Those hoping for more clarity on the political, policy or data fronts next week may be disappointed - trading could be every bit as messy and lacking in direction, especially with the end of the quarter approaching.

It's not just quarter end looming either - attention is also turning to April 2, when President Trump is expected to announce more tariffs, including reciprocal levies on many countries.

As policymakers made clear this week, the uncertainty is weighing on businesses and consumers, and potentially putting a freeze on investment, hiring and spending. Investors may decide to put their plans on ice too.

One of the strongest investment trends this year has been the reallocation of capital out of Wall Street to markets overseas. U.S. stocks have underperformed the rest of the world by around 13 percentage points.

Europe has been a particular beneficiary of these flows due to Germany's historic fiscal policy shift that may substantially boost German - and euro zone - growth. But how much juice is left in that transatlantic swing and the reversal of the 'U.S. exceptionalism' trade, at least in the near term?

European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde warned that the immediate outlook is gloomy thanks to the trade fog. And stateside, Fed officials Austan Goolsbee and John Williams on Friday drove home the stagflation warnings that the U.S. central bank made earlier in the week.

Next week promises to be just as nervy. And foggy.

I'd love to hear from you, so please reach out to me with comments at jamie.mcgeever@thomsonreuters.com. You can also follow me at @ReutersJamie and @reutersjamie.bsky.social.

[Latest Market Data segment]

This Week's Key Market Moves

  • Gold rises 1%. Remarkably, this is gold's 11th weekly gain out of the last 12, accumulating total gains of 16%.

  • The Nasdaq rises 0.5% on Friday to yield a very slender gain on the week, its first in five weeks. That averted its worst run since April-May 2022 when the index was deep in bear market territory.

  • Europe's Stoxx 600 index rises 1.2% , its biggest rise in five weeks. A final push next week, and the index is well positioned to notch its best quarter since 2020.

  • European defense stocks slip 0.5% though, their first loss in six weeks and only second in 13. They're still up 20% since Berlin's fiscal U-turn, but has the rally peaked?

  • UK 10-year gilt yields rise for a third week, and on Friday the bonds underperform French and German debt by the widest margin this year. Poor public finance figures are another headache for finance minister Rachel Reeves ahead of next week's budget update.

  • Turkey's markets plunge as concerns over the detention of President Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival persist. Stocks have their worst week since October 2008, the lira slumps 4%.

Charts of the Week

Not one, but two charts of the week this week.

The first highlights the scale of Wall Street's underperformance this year, and how quickly the 'U.S. exceptionalism' narrative has faded. Big Tech, which powered the rally in recent years, is lagging even more.

The second shows what a quarter it has been for gold bugs. The yellow metal is up 15%, its best quarter since 2016. If it can stretch that out to over 16% by March 31, it will be its best quarter since 1986.

What could move markets on Monday?

  • China BYD earnings (Q4)

  • Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) data from Japan, Germany, euro zone, UK, United States (March)

Here are some of the best things I read this week:

  • The Impact of Tariffs on Inflation - Boston Fed paper

  • American Pharmaceutical Companies Still Aren't Paying Tax in the U.S. - Brad Setser

  • Rescuing America's Economy from Trump - Brad DeLong

  • Dollar stops insulating US stocks: Mike Dolan

  • EU struggles to bring Trump to the table on tariffs

data.title: The Doctors Company’s acquisition of ProAssurance has $52.6 million termination fee
data.description:
(The Insurer) - The Doctors Company’s deal to acquire medical liability insurer ProAssurance for $1.3 billion has a termination fee of $52.6 million for both sides.

The companies announced the deal on Wednesday, which at $25 per share represents a 60% premium over ProAssurance’s $15.74 closing price on March 18.

New York-listed ProAssurance’s share price closed at $23.16 on Friday.

The deal is expected to close in the first half of 2026.

In a regulatory filing, ProAssurance said that the merger agreement contains termination rights under which under certain specified circumstances ProAssurance will be required to pay The Doctors Company a termination fee of $52.6 million.

Richard Anderson-led The Doctors Company will be required to pay ProAssurance a termination fee of $52.6 million under certain circumstances if the agreement is terminated in connection with a failure to obtain certain required regulatory approvals.

Subject to certain limitations, each of ProAssurance or The Doctors Company may terminate the merger agreement if deal is not consummated by September 19, 2026.

ProAssurance said that the deal upon close will “solidify the combined organization” as the second-largest medical malpractice insurance company in the U.S. and the largest physician-owned carrier.

In a letter sent to employees, ProAssurance president and CEO Ned Rand said: “When combined, we will be the largest physician-owned medical professional liability carrier with pro forma MPL direct written premiums of approximately $2 billion.”

The combined company will have assets of approximately $12 billion.

The Doctors Company has $7.3 billion in assets, and members surplus is over $2.8 billion.

data.title: Rithm Capital Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.25 a Share, Payable April 30 to Shareholders of Record on March 31
Brookfield Infrastructure Edging Up In US After Hours As Sells Rest Of Its Interest In US Gas Pipeline
data.description:
Brookfield Infrastructure Partners(BIP-UN.TO, BIP) edged higher in after-hours New York trade pn Friday as it agreed sell its remaining 25% interest in its U.S. natural-gas pipeline to one of the line's existing owners.

A statement noted this sale represents a complete exit of the business, generating total proceeds of over US$1.7 billion, crystalizing an "attractive" 18% Internal Rate of Return (IRR) and a three times multiple of capital on its investment since its recapitalization in 2015.

It said the transaction, when combined with the financing completed in advance of the sale, will have generated total proceeds of over $900 million in the last 18 months. The proceeds received from the sale represents a 1.8 times multiple of its current carrying value.

Friday's statement said: "We initially acquired a 27% stake in the pipeline through the purchase of Babcock & Brown Infrastructure. In 2015, we opportunistically increased our ownership to 50%, partnering with another operator to buy out a consortium of minority owners, successfully recapitalizing the business. During our ownership, we executed our business plans to create value primarily through organic growth. This included pipeline expansions into key regions to connect natural gas supply to utility providers and LNG export facilities, as well as upgrades across the existing pipeline network to increase capacity."

In further asset sale updates, Brookfield Infrastructure said it has completed the first step in the stabilized data-center monetization strategy, signing an agreement to sell a 30% interest in a 244-megawatt portfolio of de-risked and operating sites in Europe to a financial sponsor for approximately US$460 million in proceeds, approximately $90 million net to Brookfield. The company is also progressing the sale of an additional 60% stake in the portfolio, and hopes to sign off on that in coming months.

"Securing these two transactions adds to the excellent start we have had to the year. We have now locked in over $700 million in proceeds from asset sales since the beginning of the year, which is expected to increase to nearly $900 million shortly following the sell down of the additional stake in our European data center portfolio," said chief executive Sam Pollock. "This marks meaningful progress towards our asset monetization goal of $5 to $6 billion over the next two years, and we continue to experience strong activity levels and buyer interest in our in-progress capital recycling initiatives, with more to come."

Brookfield Infrastructure shares were last seen up US$0.02 to US$28.72 after hours. Its units closed up $0.04 to $41.20 on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

data.title: Forexlive Americas FX news wrap 21 Mar: Trump says there is 'flexibility' on tariffs
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump: We can talk about China tariffs
  • Canada January retail sales -0.6% vs -0.4% expected
  • Fed's Williams: Current modestly-restrictive policy is 'entirely appropriate'
  • More from Fed's Williams: We're not in a hurry to make next mon policy move
  • Eurozone March consumer confidence -14.5 vs -13.0 expected
  • Fed's Goolsbee: Business contacts are waiting on capital spending in light of tariffs
  • What FedEx earnings reveal about the state of the economy
  • ECB's Stournaras: All available information points to another rate cut in April
  • Fed's Waller: I preferred to continue current pace of balance sheet runoff
  • King Charles made an offer to Trump to join the Commonwealth, hoping to cool tensions
  • Canada February new housing price index 0.1% versus -0.1% last month
  • Trump unswayed at efforts to craft tariff deal - report
  • Bakers Hughes oil rig count -1 at 486

Markets:

  • WTI crude oil up 20-cents to $68.27
  • US 10-year yields up 1.7 bps to 4.25%
  • Gold down $22 to $3021
  • S&P 500 up 0.1%, Nasdaq up 0.5%
  • USD leads, AUD lags

It was a strange day in markets and I suspect that the quad witching had something to do with it. Equities were beaten up early but came back to life when Trump said there was 'flexibility' on tariffs ahead of April 2 and that he was going to talk with Xi. Prior to that the US dollar was broadly strong but it gave some back afterwards.

Aside from that, it looked like flows were in charge. The euro and pound bottomed out right at the European close in a flurry of USD strength. That partly unwound later but still left the dollar solidly higher on the day in a reversal of the recent trend. The euro touched 1.0798 from a high of 1.0861 in Europe. It bounced from the figure to end at 1.0815.

The Canadian dollar was in focus with retail sales data released. USD/CAD rose after the data as it modestly missed estimates and hit 1.4375 but the advance reading for February sales was better than feared and that move unwound along with the better risk mood and a bounce in oil prices.

Gold hit an air pocket shortly after the US equity open in a quick fall to $3000 from $3035. Bids at the figure held though and that started a slow rebounded to $3023 last.

Overall, it wasn't a big day for news or market moves. The comments from Fed officials highlighted the uncertainty on the outlook and a willingness to wait on economic data. That was totally in-line with what Powell said on Wednesday.

Have a great weekend.

data.title: Smith Douglas Homes Corp. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Smith Douglas Homes Corp., a prominent homebuilder operating in high-growth markets in the Southeastern and Southern United States, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Home closing revenue: $975.5 million, an increase of 27.6% from the previous year, driven by a 24.8% increase in homes closed and a 2.1% increase in the average selling price of homes closed.
  • Home closing gross profit: $255.5 million, an increase of 18.1% from the previous year, with a gross margin of 26.2%, down from 28.3% due to a 5.2% increase in the average cost of homes closed.
  • Net income: $111.8 million, a decrease of 9.2% from the previous year, primarily due to increased selling, general, and administrative costs and income tax expenses following the IPO.
  • Net income attributable to Smith Douglas Homes Corp.: $16.1 million, reflecting the portion of net income after the IPO and Reorganization Transactions.
  • Basic earnings per share: $1.82, calculated for the period from January 11, 2024, to December 31, 2024.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $1.81, reflecting the impact of potential share dilution.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The company operates in high-growth markets in the Southeastern and Southern United States, with operations organized into eight geographical divisions across two segments: Southeast (Atlanta, Central Georgia, Charlotte, Greenville, Raleigh) and Central (Alabama, Houston, Nashville). Each market is experiencing strong housing demand, providing significant opportunities for expansion.
  • Sales Units: In 2024, the company closed 2,867 homes, marking a 24.8% increase from 2023. The average selling price (ASP) of homes closed was approximately $340,000, reflecting a 2.1% increase from the previous year.
  • New Market Entry: The company entered the Houston market in 2023 through the acquisition of Devon Street Homes and expanded into the Greenville market in 2024, continuing to build infrastructure around Atlanta by expanding into Central Georgia and Chattanooga.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company maintains a highly efficient homebuilding production model with an average construction cycle time of approximately 55 business days, excluding the Houston division. This efficiency contributes to strong home closing gross margins.
  • Land Acquisition Strategy: The company employs a land-light business model, primarily acquiring finished lots through lot-option contracts, which reduces upfront capital requirements and aligns with the pace of home orders and starts. As of December 31, 2024, 96% of unstarted controlled lots were controlled through lot option contracts.
  • Customer Segments: The company targets entry-level and empty-nest homebuyers, offering a personalized home buying experience at affordable price points. The ASP of homes closed was below Federal Housing Administration (FHA) loan limits, making them attractive to the target demographic.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to capitalize on its land-light strategy to efficiently build new communities and drive superior risk-adjusted returns. It aims to increase market share within existing markets and opportunistically expand into new markets, particularly in the Southeastern and Southern United States.
  • Technology Integration: The company utilizes SMART Builder, a real-time, schedule-driven ERP system, to manage construction processes and enhance operational efficiency. This system supports the Rteam process, which fosters collaboration and accountability among business partners.
  • Community Expansion: As of December 31, 2024, the company had 78 active communities, a 13% increase from the previous year, with average monthly sales per community slightly decreasing from 3.4 to 3.0.
  • Customer Satisfaction: The company achieved low cancellation rates of 12% in 2024, attributed to its differentiated value proposition and efficient construction cycle times, which enhance homebuyer satisfaction.

Strategic Initiatives

  • IPO and Capital Management: Smith Douglas Homes Corp. successfully completed its IPO, issuing 8,846,154 shares of Class A common stock, raising approximately $172.8 million in net proceeds. The company used the IPO proceeds to purchase newly issued LLC Interests and repay $84.0 million of borrowings under its Prior Credit Facility. It also redeemed all outstanding Class C and D Units of Smith Douglas Holdings LLC and repaid notes payable to related parties.
  • Amended Credit Facility: The Amended Credit Facility increased the revolving credit commitments to $250.0 million, with a $100.0 million accordion feature, and extended the maturity date to January 16, 2027. The company maintains a prudent cash management strategy, focusing on lot acquisitions and construction costs.
  • Future Outlook: Smith Douglas Homes Corp. plans to continue its strategic focus on entry-level and empty-nest homebuyers, with an emphasis on affordable luxury. The company anticipates using its existing cash reserves, generated cash flows, and credit facility availability to fund ongoing operations and future lot purchases. It remains open to seeking additional capital to enhance liquidity and support long-term growth strategies, including potential market expansions and acquisitions.

Challenges and Risks

  • Economic and Market Risks: The company faces significant risks due to the cyclical nature of the housing industry, which is heavily influenced by changes in economic conditions such as interest rates, inflation, and supply chain disruptions. The availability and cost of financing for homebuyers, as well as changes in federal and state tax laws, can materially impact the company's operations. Additionally, geopolitical instability, such as the Russia-Ukraine conflict and the Israel-Hamas conflict, along with global trade tensions, pose risks to the financial markets and consumer confidence.
  • Operational Risks: The company is vulnerable to fluctuations in mortgage interest rates, which have increased substantially between 2022 and 2024, affecting consumer affordability and potentially reducing demand for new homes. Regulatory changes in mortgage lending practices could further restrict the availability of financing, impacting the company's sales and financial condition. The company also relies on trade partners for construction, which could be affected by labor shortages, increased costs, or improper construction practices. Competition from other homebuilders could impact its market share and profitability.
  • Environmental Risks: Natural and man-made disasters, severe weather, and climate change potentially increase costs and cause project delays. The company is also subject to environmental, health, and safety regulations, which could result in additional compliance costs or liabilities.
  • Geographic Concentration: The company's geographic concentration, particularly in Atlanta, Georgia, could disproportionately impact its business if the local housing market declines. Management is aware of the potential impact of inflation on construction costs and financing and is monitoring these factors closely.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks related to interest rate changes, which could affect its borrowing costs and financial results. The lack of hedging against interest rate fluctuations presents a risk, although management may consider obtaining interest rate protection in the future. Additionally, the company is subject to risks associated with real estate value fluctuations, which could require write-downs of its real estate assets.

SEC Filing: Smith Douglas Homes Corp. [ SDHC ] - 10-K - Mar. 21, 2025

data.title: Salarius Pharmaceuticals, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Salarius Pharmaceuticals, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing treatments for cancers caused by dysregulated gene expression, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces.

Financial Highlights

Net Loss: $5.58 million. The company reported a significant reduction in net loss compared to the previous year, primarily due to decreased operating expenses.

Loss per Common Share — Basic and Diluted: $5.79. This reflects the company's ongoing financial challenges and the impact of reduced operating expenses.

Total Operating Expenses: $5.73 million. A substantial decrease from the previous year, driven by cost-saving measures and reduced research and development spending.

Research and Development Expenses: $0.77 million. A significant reduction from the previous year, attributed to the implementation of a cost savings plan and lower spending on clinical trials.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: Salarius Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing treatments for cancers caused by dysregulated gene expression. The company is working on two classes of drugs: targeted protein inhibitors and targeted protein degraders, with potential applications in both liquid and solid tumors.

Product Pipeline: The current pipeline includes two small molecule drugs: SP-3164, a targeted protein degrader, and seclidemstat (SP-2577), a targeted protein inhibitor. SP-3164 is being developed for high unmet need hematological indications and solid tumors, while SP-2577 is being evaluated for Ewing sarcoma and other conditions.

Clinical Trials: Salarius has been involved in clinical trials for SP-2577, including a Phase 1/2 trial for Ewing sarcoma, which was closed to conserve cash. The company continues to support a trial at MD Anderson Cancer Center evaluating SP-2577 in combination with azacytidine for myelodysplastic syndromes and chronic myelomonocytic leukemia.

Regulatory Developments: SP-2577 has received FDA fast track designation as a potential treatment for a rare pediatric disease in Ewing’s Sarcoma. The FDA placed a partial clinical hold on a trial due to a serious adverse event, which was later lifted after addressing FDA's questions.

Operational Adjustments: Due to financial constraints, Salarius has implemented cost-saving measures, including curtailing spending on clinical trials and reducing operational expenses. The company is relying on data from investigator-initiated trials to guide future development plans.

Future Outlook: Salarius is focused on completing the merger with Decoy Therapeutics and exploring strategic alternatives to maximize stockholder value. The company is also considering raising additional capital to fund ongoing operations and development activities.

Strategic Initiatives

Merger Agreement: Salarius Pharmaceuticals entered into a Merger Agreement with Decoy Therapeutics, aiming to combine operations and enhance strategic positioning. The merger is structured as a stock-for-stock transaction, with Decoy stockholders expected to own approximately 85.9% of the combined entity. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to explore strategic alternatives, including acquisitions, mergers, and other transactions to maximize stockholder value.

Capital Management: The company has been actively managing its capital through various financing activities. It entered into a securities purchase agreement with C/M Capital Master Fund, LP, allowing the sale of up to $10 million in newly issued shares. Additionally, Salarius executed a Warrant Cancellation Agreement, paying $350,000 to cancel a warrant for 454,546 shares. The company also issued and sold shares under an At the Market Offering Agreement, raising $1.7 million in 2024. These actions are part of efforts to manage liquidity and extend the cash runway.

Future Outlook: Salarius faces substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to recurring losses and lack of revenue from product sales. The company plans to rely on the merger with Decoy Therapeutics to improve its financial position. If the merger does not close, Salarius may need to pursue additional capital raising activities or consider winding down operations. The company is also considering a reverse stock split to maintain Nasdaq listing requirements and improve stock liquidity.

Challenges and Risks

Financial Position: Salarius faces significant challenges due to its current financial position, lack of revenue from product sales, and reliance on external funding to continue operations. The recent decision to close the clinical trial for SP-2577 in Ewing sarcoma highlights the financial constraints impacting the company's ability to advance its pipeline.

Merger Risks: The proposed merger with Decoy Therapeutics, Inc. introduces several risks, including potential dilution of Salarius stockholders' ownership, the possibility of not realizing anticipated strategic and financial benefits, and the risk of the merger not being completed due to unmet conditions.

Nasdaq Listing: The company also faces the risk of delisting from Nasdaq due to non-compliance with listing requirements, which could severely impact liquidity and access to capital.

Operational Risks: Salarius' reliance on third-party manufacturers and collaborators for clinical trials and product development poses operational risks.

Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks include the uncertainty of obtaining necessary approvals for product candidates and the potential impact of changes in patent law on the company's intellectual property strategy.

Management's Discussion: Management acknowledges the financial challenges, including the need for additional capital to fund operations beyond the second quarter of 2025. The company is exploring strategic alternatives, including the merger with Decoy, to enhance stockholder value. However, the success of these strategies is uncertain, and failure to secure necessary financing or complete the merger could lead to significant operational disruptions.

Market Risks: Salarius is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in stock price and potential delisting from Nasdaq, which could affect its ability to raise capital. The company's financial condition is further impacted by its accumulated deficit and ongoing net losses, necessitating careful management of cash resources and strategic planning to mitigate these risks.

SEC Filing: Salarius Pharmaceuticals, Inc. [ SLRX ] - 10-K - Mar. 21, 2025

data.title: NORDSTROM INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Nordstrom, Inc., a leading fashion retailer known for its high-quality merchandise across apparel, shoes, beauty, accessories, and home goods, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report details the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces as it prepares for a potential merger that would take it private.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $14,557 million, a 2.4% increase compared with 2023, driven by strong growth in women's apparel, active, men's apparel, kids, and shoes.
  • Gross Profit: $5,161 million, an increase of $245 million and 90 basis points as a rate of net sales compared with 2023, primarily due to higher sales and merchandise margin expansion.
  • Earnings Before Interest and Income Taxes (EBIT): $495 million, an increase of $244 million and 165 basis points as a rate of net sales in 2024 compared with 2023, reflecting higher sales and improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Earnings: $294 million, a significant increase from $134 million in 2023, reflecting improved sales and operational performance.
  • Diluted EPS: $1.74, an increase of $0.92 in 2024 compared with 2023, primarily due to higher sales and improved financial results.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Nordstrom operates as a leading fashion retailer with a focus on providing high-quality merchandise across apparel, shoes, beauty, accessories, and home goods. The company leverages both Nordstrom and Nordstrom Rack banners to offer a wide selection of products and services, including alterations, order pickup, dining, and styling, to enhance customer experience.
  • Operational Segments: Nordstrom operates under one reportable segment that aggregates its two operating segments: Nordstrom and Nordstrom Rack. Nordstrom is a full-price retailer, while Nordstrom Rack serves as an off-price destination, offering clearance merchandise and products from the same brands carried at Nordstrom.
  • Store and Digital Presence: As of February 1, 2025, Nordstrom operates 92 Nordstrom stores, six Nordstrom Locals, and 277 Nordstrom Rack stores. The company also maintains a strong digital presence through Nordstrom.com, NordstromRack.com, and mobile applications.
  • Digital Marketplace Launch: In April 2024, Nordstrom launched a digital Marketplace to expand its online assortment using unowned inventory models, allowing for a greater selection of products, brands, and sizes.
  • Nordstrom Rack Expansion: In 2024, Nordstrom Rack opened 23 new stores across the U.S., with plans to open a similar number in 2025. These stores serve as a primary source of new customers who often transition to the Nordstrom banner.
  • Omni-Channel Capabilities: Nordstrom has enhanced its omni-channel platform by integrating store fulfillment of Rack digital orders and enabling buy online, pick up in store services in over 100 Nordstrom Rack locations.
  • Supply Chain Network: The company operates two fulfillment centers, six distribution centers, and one omni-channel center to support its omni-channel capabilities, providing greater access to merchandise selection and faster delivery.
  • Employee and Culture Initiatives: Nordstrom is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace culture, supporting employee well-being, and offering growth and development opportunities. The company employs approximately 55,000 people, with a focus on inclusion and belonging.
  • Corporate Social Responsibility: Nordstrom's CSR strategy focuses on environmental sustainability, product sustainability, human rights, and corporate philanthropy. Key initiatives include the BEAUTYCYCLE program, gender equity in production facilities, and charitable partnerships.
  • Future Outlook: Nordstrom expects continued growth and momentum in 2025, with plans to further expand its Nordstrom Rack footprint and enhance its digital and omni-channel capabilities. The company is also preparing for a potential merger that would take it private, subject to shareholder approval.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: In 2024, Nordstrom focused on driving growth through digital-led initiatives, operational optimization, and expanding Nordstrom Rack stores. The company launched a digital Marketplace on Nordstrom.com to enhance customer shopping experiences and continued to optimize its supply chain for improved efficiency and customer service. Additionally, Nordstrom announced a pending merger with members of the Nordstrom family and Liverpool to take the company private, which is expected to close in the first half of 2025.
  • Capital Management: Nordstrom ended fiscal year 2024 with $1,035 million in cash and cash equivalents and $771 million of additional liquidity available on its Revolver. The company retired its 2.30% senior notes due in April 2024 using cash on hand. Nordstrom did not repurchase any shares in 2024 due to restrictions under the Merger Agreement, which also limits dividend declarations to regular quarterly dividends not exceeding $0.19 per share. The company paid dividends of $124 million, or $0.76 per share, in 2024.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Nordstrom expects to continue its momentum in 2025 with a focus on profitable growth and customer experience enhancement. The company plans to open a similar number of Nordstrom Rack stores in 2025 as in 2024 and aims to scale its Marketplace business. Capital expenditures are expected to range from 2.5% to 3.5% of net sales, primarily supporting technology and store investments. The pending merger is anticipated to close in the first half of 2025, after which Nordstrom will become a private company.

Challenges and Risks

  • Merger Transition Risks: The company is undergoing a significant transition with a proposed merger that will take it private. This transition involves various risks, including potential disruptions to business operations, employee distractions, and changes in business relationships. The merger is expected to close in the first half of 2025, subject to shareholder approval and other conditions.
  • Strategic and Operational Risks: The company faces strategic and operational risks, including the need to successfully execute its customer strategy and adapt to competitive market forces. The retail environment is rapidly evolving, with increasing competition from non-traditional retailers and changing customer preferences. The company must also manage inventory levels effectively to respond to fashion trends and consumer demand.
  • Merger Agreement Risks: The merger agreement introduces specific risks, such as potential litigation, restrictions on business activities, and the possibility that the merger may not be completed within the expected timeframe. Additionally, the concentration of stock ownership in a small number of shareholders may limit the ability to influence corporate matters.
  • Data and Cybersecurity Risks: Data, cybersecurity, and information technology risks are significant, with the company and its third-party providers handling sensitive data subject to evolving privacy and security laws. The company has implemented measures to safeguard its information, but these cannot completely eliminate cybersecurity risks.
  • Operational Challenges: The company is focused on driving growth at the Nordstrom banner, optimizing operations, and building momentum at Nordstrom Rack. However, it faces challenges such as higher labor costs, supply chain disruptions, and the need to maintain inventory integrity. The company is investing in technology and supply chain improvements to enhance customer experience and operational efficiency.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations and changes in credit and capital markets. These could increase the cost of financing or restrict access to liquidity. The company is managing its cash and capital structure to maximize shareholder return and maintain financial flexibility.

SEC Filing: NORDSTROM INC [ JWN ] - 10-K - Mar. 21, 2025

data.title: Moleculin Biotech, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Moleculin Biotech, Inc., a late-stage pharmaceutical development company, has released its 2024 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The company is primarily focused on the development of Annamycin, a non-cardiotoxic anthracycline, for the treatment of relapsed/refractory acute myeloid leukemia (AML) and soft tissue sarcoma (STS) lung metastases.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 million. The company did not generate any revenue for the years ended December 31, 2024, and 2023.
  • Operating Expenses: $26.6 million. Operating expenses decreased from $29.6 million in 2023 to $26.6 million in 2024, primarily due to reductions in research and development and general and administrative expenses.
  • Loss from Operations: $(26.6) million. The company reported a loss from operations, reflecting the absence of revenue and high operating expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(21.8) million. The net loss for 2024 was reduced compared to 2023, primarily due to a gain from the change in fair value of warrant liability.
  • Interest Income, Net: $0.55 million. Interest income decreased due to a lower cash balance and decreasing interest rates.
  • Gain (Loss) from Change in Fair Value of Warrant Liability: $6.1 million. A significant gain was recorded in 2024 compared to a loss in 2023, driven by a decline in the company's share price.

Business Highlights

  • Business Focus: The company is primarily focused on the development of Annamycin for the treatment of relapsed/refractory AML and STS lung metastases.
  • Clinical Trials: Moleculin is conducting a pivotal Phase 3 trial for Annamycin in combination with Cytarabine for AML, with interim data expected by the end of 2025 and additional data in the first half of 2026. The trial is part of the MIRACLE study, a global initiative.
  • Product Development: Annamycin has shown significant efficacy in Phase 1 and 2 trials, with complete remission rates above current second-line therapies for AML and comparable survival rates for STS lung metastases.
  • Regulatory Designations: Annamycin has received Orphan Drug Designation and Fast Track status from the FDA for both AML and STS, which could expedite its development and review process.
  • Geographical Performance: The company is conducting clinical trials in multiple regions, including the US, Europe, Western Asia, and the Middle East, indicating a broad geographical focus for its operations.
  • Future Outlook: Moleculin plans to focus on advancing Annamycin through clinical trials and aims to leverage its non-cardiotoxic profile to potentially disrupt the competitive landscape for AML and STS treatments.
  • Research Collaborations: The company collaborates with academic institutions for clinical and preclinical research, with a focus on externally funded trials to advance its drug candidates.
  • Operational Strategy: Moleculin operates in a primarily virtual environment with headquarters in Houston, Texas, and relies on third-party manufacturing and potential outlicensing or collaborative opportunities for marketing and distribution.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Focus: Moleculin is primarily focused on the development of its lead drug candidate, Annamycin, for the treatment of relapsed/refractory AML and STS lung metastases. The company is conducting a pivotal Phase 3 trial for Annamycin, with interim data expected by the end of 2025.
  • Capital Management: The company raised $9.3 million in gross proceeds from financing activities in the first quarter of 2025, including the sale of common stock and warrants. In 2024, Moleculin also raised $5.5 million through a securities purchase agreement and warrant amendment. The company has not paid any dividends and does not anticipate declaring any in the foreseeable future. Moleculin's cash resources as of December 31, 2024, along with recent financing, are expected to fund operations into the third quarter of 2025.
  • Future Outlook: Moleculin plans to continue focusing on the development of Annamycin and advancing its other drug candidates through investigator-led studies. The company aims to secure additional funding through various means, including equity offerings, debt financings, and strategic alliances, to support its operations and clinical trials. Moleculin is also addressing an ongoing SEC investigation related to its COVID-19 drug candidate, which may impact its liquidity and capital resources.

Challenges and Risks

  • Regulatory Risks: The company faces significant regulatory risks related to the development and commercialization of its drug candidates. The lengthy and unpredictable nature of the FDA and foreign regulatory approval processes could delay or prevent the commercialization of products, impacting revenue generation.
  • Operational Risks: Moleculin relies on third-party manufacturers for active pharmaceutical ingredients (API), which introduces risks related to supply chain disruptions and compliance with cGMP regulations. The potential for severe adverse outcomes in clinical trials could negatively impact regulatory approval and commercial acceptance.
  • Intellectual Property Risks: The composition of matter patent for Annamycin has expired, and other patents may not be issued. The company's intellectual property rights are also subject to the rights of the US government, which could impair their value.
  • Financial Risks: The company requires substantial additional funding to complete clinical development and commercialize its products. The uncertainty in successful product development makes it difficult to estimate the actual amount of funding required, posing a risk to ongoing operations.
  • Market Risks: Moleculin is exposed to market risks, including foreign currency fluctuations that could increase operating expenses. Additionally, the potential for new or increased tariffs poses a risk to the company's supply chain and financial condition.

SEC Filing: Moleculin Biotech, Inc. [ MBRX ] - 10-K - Mar. 21, 2025

data.title: Affinity Bancshares, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Affinity Bancshares, Inc., operating primarily through its wholly owned subsidiary, Affinity Bank, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. Affinity Bank, headquartered in Covington, Georgia, focuses on taking deposits from the public and investing in various loan types, including commercial real estate, commercial and industrial, and residential real estate loans.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $47.973 million, reflecting a 12.3% increase from the previous year due to higher interest income on loans.
  • Net Interest Income Before Provision for Credit Losses: $29.166 million, a 7.2% increase from the prior year, driven by a higher net interest rate spread.
  • Net Income: $5.441 million, a decrease of 15.6% compared to the previous year, primarily due to increased interest and noninterest expenses.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $0.85, down from $1.00 in the previous year, reflecting the decrease in net income.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $0.83, compared to $0.98 in the prior year, indicating a decline in profitability per share.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Affinity Bancshares, Inc. operates primarily through its wholly owned subsidiary, Affinity Bank, a national bank headquartered in Covington, Georgia. The company focuses on taking deposits from the public and investing in various loan types, including commercial real estate, commercial and industrial, and residential real estate loans.
  • Market Area: The company conducts operations from its main office and branch offices in Georgia, with a commercial loan production office in Alpharetta, Georgia. It also gathers deposits nationwide through its virtual bank, FitnessBank.
  • Loan Portfolio Composition: As of December 31, 2024, the loan portfolio was primarily composed of commercial real estate loans (45.3%), commercial and industrial loans (20.8%), and consumer installment loans (16.9%).
  • Specialized Lending: Affinity Bank has specialized expertise in lending to dentists and dental practices, with loans to the dental industry totaling $185.4 million, or 26.0% of the loan portfolio.
  • Geographical Performance: The company holds a significant market share of deposits in Newton County, Georgia, representing 19.83% of FDIC-insured deposits, ranking first among eight institutions.
  • New Product Launches: The company offers a variety of deposit accounts, including checking, savings, and certificates of deposit, and gathers deposits through FitnessBank, which provides higher interest rates based on customers meeting fitness goals.
  • Competition: Affinity Bank faces competition from large money center and regional banks, community banks, credit unions, and other financial institutions in its local market area.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to enhance its products and services to meet the changing needs of customers, focusing on expanding relationships with current customers and developing new, profitable business relationships.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Interest Rate Risk Management: The company has implemented strategies to manage interest rate risk, including limiting reliance on non-core funding sources, growing transaction deposit accounts, increasing the investment securities portfolio with an average maturity of less than 15 years, diversifying the loan portfolio by adding more commercial-related loans, and encouraging borrowers to select adjustable rate loans.
  • Liquidity Enhancement: The company has focused on enhancing liquidity through obtaining brokered deposits and increasing Federal Home Loan Bank advances.
  • Capital Management: The company declared a special cash dividend of $1.50 per share to be paid on March 27, 2025, and adopted a stock repurchase program allowing for the repurchase of up to 320,480 shares of its common stock, approximately 5% of the current outstanding shares. Stockholders’ equity increased by $7.6 million or 6.3% due to net income, changes in accumulated other comprehensive loss, and stock compensation expenses. The company also increased its borrowings to enhance liquidity, with $54.0 million in Federal Home Loan Bank advances and $4.8 million under the Bank Term Funding Program.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to maintain a strong liquidity position and anticipates having sufficient funds to meet current funding commitments. It expects to retain a significant portion of maturing time deposits and will continue to monitor its liquidity position on a daily basis. The company is committed to maintaining compliance with regulatory capital requirements and is categorized as well-capitalized. Future strategic initiatives will likely focus on further diversifying the loan portfolio and managing interest rate risk effectively.

Challenges and Risks

  • Competition: Affinity Bancshares, Inc. faces significant competition within its local market area from a variety of financial institutions, including large money center and regional banks, community banks, and credit unions. This competition affects both loan origination and deposit attraction, potentially impacting the company's market share and profitability.
  • Economic Conditions: Adverse conditions in the financial markets and the broader economy, both nationally and in specific market areas, could negatively impact the company's performance.
  • Credit Risk: Changes in loan delinquencies, write-offs, and the adequacy of the allowance for credit losses could affect financial stability.
  • Funding and Liquidity: The company's ability to access cost-effective funding and maintain liquidity is crucial for ongoing operations.
  • Real Estate Market: Fluctuations in real estate values and market conditions for both residential and commercial properties could impact loan demand and asset quality.
  • Regulatory Changes: Changes in laws, government regulations, and policies affecting financial institutions could impose additional compliance costs and operational constraints.
  • Technological Risks: The company faces risks related to technological changes, cybersecurity threats, and the performance of third-party providers.
  • Environmental and Geopolitical Risks: Climate change, domestic and international hostilities, and public health emergencies could have unforeseen impacts on operations.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the competitive landscape and economic uncertainties. Strategies to address these include enhancing product offerings, focusing on customer relationships, and leveraging technology to improve operational efficiency. The company also emphasizes the importance of maintaining strong capital and liquidity positions to navigate potential economic downturns.
  • Interest Rate Risk: Affinity Bancshares, Inc. is exposed to interest rate risk, which could affect its net interest margin and overall financial performance. The company actively manages this risk through its asset-liability management strategies, including the use of interest rate swaps and other hedging instruments to mitigate potential adverse effects of interest rate fluctuations.

data.title: Dow, S&P end flat, Nasdaq snaps four-week decline on tariff hopes
data.description:
Key points:
  • Nike dips on bleak revenue outlook
  • FedEx tumbles after results, outlook
  • Boeing jumps after fighter jet contract award
  • Indexes up: Dow 0.08%, S&P 0.08%, Nasdaq 0.52%

The S&P and Dow eked out slight gains on Friday, erasing earlier losses after comments from U.S. President Donald Trump provided hope that previously announced tariffs expected to begin in early April may not be as burdensome as feared.

Trump said there will be flexibility on tariffs and that his top trade chief plans to speak with his Chinese counterpart next week. The president also reiterated his plan to use duties as a way to narrow the U.S. trade deficit with China.

Markets have been under pressure in recent weeks as changing announcements about the timing and size of tariffs have clouded the outlook for corporate profits as well as the Federal Reserve's monetary policy path.

Stocks have shown some signs of bottoming this week, however, with the S&P climbing more than 1% on Wednesday in the wake of the Fed's latest policy announcement. The central bank kept rates unchanged and signaled two cuts were likely this year.

Even so, Michael Arone, chief investment strategist for the U.S. SPDR Business at State Street Global Advisors in Boston, said it was concerning that investors' attempts to rally the stock market had largely failed.

"The reasons are the continued uncertainty around trade policy from the Trump administration, continued concerns about a U.S. economic growth scare and ultimately uncertainty about what the path of monetary policy looks like," Arone said.

Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said the central bank needs more time to "sort through" how Trump's policies play out in the economy, while New York Fed President John Williams echoed Goolsbee's comments and said there was no rush to change monetary policy right now.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 32.03 points, or 0.08%, to 41,985.35, the S&P 500 gained 4.67 points, or 0.08%, to 5,667.56 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 92.43 points, or 0.52%, to 17,784.05.

The S&P was down as much as 1.06% earlier in the day.

For the week, the S&P 500 gained 0.5%, the Nasdaq rose 0.17%, and the Dow climbed 1.2%. It was the largest weekly gain for the Dow in two months, while the Nasdaq and S&P 500 managed to barely snap four-week streaks of declines.

With earnings season set to begin next month, multiple companies have been reducing their forecasts. FedEx slumped 6.45% after the package delivery company cut its full-year profit and revenue forecasts, citing continued weakness and uncertainty in the U.S. industrial economy.

Peer UPS declined 1.61%. Delivery firms are often seen as a bellwether for the global economy given their reach into a wide swath of different industries.

The delivery companies weighed on the Dow Jones Transport Index , which dropped as much as 2.7% during the session before closing down only 0.2%.

Nike slid 5.46% as the worst performer among Dow Industrial components after the sports apparel maker projected a sharper decline in fourth-quarter revenue than analysts had anticipated.

The materials sector, off 1% on the session, was weighed down by a 5.78% tumble in shares of Nucor Corp after the company forecast first-quarter profit below estimates.

Boeing climbed 3.06% after Trump awarded the planemaker a contract to build the U.S. Air Force's most sophisticated fighter jet, beating out rival Lockheed Martin , which slumped 5.79%.

Friday's session also marked the simultaneous expiry of quarterly derivatives contracts tied to stocks, index options and futures, also known as "triple witching", which can exacerbate market volatility.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.93-to-1 ratio on the New York Stock Exchange and by a 1.42-to-1 ratio on Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted nine new 52-week highs and 16 new lows, while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 38 new highs and 188 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was 21.05 billion shares, compared with the 16.47 billion average for the full session over the last 20 trading days, and the busiest trading day since January 7.

data.title: Latam currencies pressured by firm dollar after week of rate decisions
data.description:
Key points:
  • Latam stocks, FX off 0.4% each
  • Russian central bank holds rates at 21%
  • Turkish assets continue to slide
  • Colombia's peso worst hit in Latam this week

Most Latin American currencies slipped on Friday as the dollar remained firm, wrapping up a week packed with interest rate decisions, including in Brazil and the United States.

The dollar index held on to its gains heading into the weekend after the U.S. Federal Reserve on Wednesday indicated that it saw no rush to cut interest rates.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell said U.S. President Donald Trump's initial policies, including extensive import tariffs, appear to have tilted the U.S. economy toward slower growth and at least temporarily higher inflation.

Trump hinted at some flexibility on tariffs on Friday, though the details remained unclear.

The higher dollar weighed on Latam currencies, with the Brazilian real leading losses in the region. It fell 0.6% and was set for a slight weekly decline.

Brazil's central bank raised interest rates by 100 basis points, but signaled smaller hikes going forward.

The Mexican peso lost 0.5%, set for its biggest weekly loss since late January.

Chile's peso weakened 0.2% ahead of an interest rate decision later in the day, with consensus tilted toward the central bank keeping its main lending rate at 5%.

"With inflation leveling off in Chile just below 5% - against an inflation target of 3% - further rate cuts are unlikely in 2025," said Thierry Wizman, global FX & rates strategist at Macquarie.

However, Citigroup analysts said, "we believe the central bank could still opt for a total 50bps in cuts in 2025, which we pencil in as two 25bps in the last two quarters of the year.

"Delays in Fed cuts could delay Chile's central bank moves, or lead to a change of guidance towards a higher terminal rate."

Colombia's peso bucked the trend, gaining 0.7%, but was on pace to be the worst performing currency in Latin America this week.

The currency was hit hard after Colombian Finance Minister Diego Guevara announced his resignation on Tuesday, amid clashes over budget cuts and just hours after lawmakers rejected a labor reform championed by leftist President Gustavo Petro.

Meanwhile, a report said the International Monetary Fund will discuss a new $20-billion loan for Argentina in an informal meeting next week. The peso was flat.

Uncertainty around U.S. trade policy and worries of an American economic slowdown have induced volatility in global markets.

MSCI's index for Latin American stocks (.MILA00000PUS) was down 0.4%, along with the currencies index (.MILA00000CUS), which was on track to log its first weekly loss in three weeks.

Regional stocks were mixed, while Argentina's Merval gained 2.5%.

On a year-to-date basis, Brazil's real stands as the best performing currency among emerging markets, while Turkey's lira and the Indonesian rupiah have fared the worst.

Turkish assets sold off for a third straight day, triggering two market-wide circuit breakers on Borsa Istanbul, as the lira, equities, and bonds extended losses following the detention of President Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival on Wednesday.

Debt markets in other frontier countries were also under pressure, with long-dated dollar bonds in Kenya and Egypt sliding more than 1 cent on the dollar, Tradeweb data showed.

The Russian central bank kept its benchmark interest rate on hold at 21% as expected and said more rate hikes were possible as inflationary pressures have decreased but remain high.

data.title: ParaZero Announces 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
TEL AVIV, Israel, March 21, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — ParaZero Technologies Ltd. (the “company” or “ParaZero”), an aerospace company focused on safety systems for commercial unmanned aircrafts and defense Counter UAS systems, reported today its financial results for year ended December 31, 2024.

Key Highlights of ParaZero’s Achievements in 2024 & Recent Highlights:

  • Sales increased by 50.2% to $932,154 for the year ended December 31, 2024, mainly thanks to shifting towards sales to OEM integrations, which couples with recuring revenues in addition to the traditional aftermarket segment.
  • Since the beginning of 2025, the Company has significantly strengthened its cash position by completing a registered direct offering of aggregate gross proceeds of approximately $3.1 million, in addition to receiving $1.3 million in proceeds from warrant exercises.

Expanded operations in the defense sector & Recent Highlights:

  • During 2024, the company enhanced its penetration to the defense market with new developments and collaborations. The company launched its DropAir - Precision Airdrop System for rapid delivery of blood transfusions in high-risk operational zones. ParaZero successfully tested the system in collaboration with a leading drone company and moved forward to phase II of the project with Israeli Ministry of Defense.
  • The company continued to advance its Counter-Unmanned Aerial Systems (C-UAS) solution, securing new orders, including from a Tier-1 defense customer.
  • Both systems gained approvals from the Israeli Defense Export Controls Agency (DECA), under the Israel Ministry of Defense (MOD).

Commercial developments:

ParaZero continued to establish its position as a leader in safety systems for urban mobility and aerial vehicles.

  • The company launched number of new collaborations that led to new orders, including a $187,000 purchase order from a U.S.-based Advanced Air Mobility (AAM) company.
  • In addition, it successfully delivered a special drone safety system project for a leading Fortune 500 automotive manufacturer as part of the customer's proprietary drone program.
  • ParaZero received a significant order from a leading Australian distributor, strengthening its presence in Australia.
  • ParaZero announced its expansion in the U.S. through collaboration with one of North America’s largest drone distributors, Drone Nerds, to promote and sell ParaZero’s products.

Regulatory approval:

The Company continues to facilitate its customers in achieving regulatory approvals for their operations as well as securing new approvals for its solutions.

  • ParaZero received the prestigious Design Verification Report (DVR) approval from the European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) for its SafeAir M-300 Pro and SafeAir M-350 Pro parachute safety kits. Its SafeAir systems, integrated with the DJI Matrice 350, DJI Mavic 3T and DJI Mavic 3E, have successfully achieved CE Class C5 compliance.
  • The company also announced that a leading drone solutions provider, utilizing ParaZero’s advanced safety technology, secured a Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) waiver to conduct Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) drone operations.

“2024 was a year of advancements for ParaZero, as recent geopolitical events have demonstrated the growing reliance on drones in modern warfare, both as offensive tools and as critical assets for delivering essential supplies in high-risk operational zones. At ParaZero, we have spent over a decade developing and refining our innovative, patent-protected technologies to address these evolving threats,” stated Boaz Shetzer, CEO of ParaZero. “I am proud of the company’s ability to successfully translate our innovation to meet the new needs of our customers and the market.”

Shetzer added, “Our commitment to enhancing operational efficiency and safety is evident in our recent accomplishments, including strategic collaborations in the defense, urban mobility and aerial vehicle sectors, the successful advancement of our new DropAir Precision Airdrop System, the late-stage development of our C-UAS solution and our leadership in regulatory achievements.”  

Full Year 2024 Financial Highlights:

  • Sales increased by $311,646, or 50.2%, to $932,154 for the year ended December 31, 2024, compared to $620,508 for the year ended December 31, 2023. This increase was mainly attributed to that fact, that the company shifted towards sales to OEMs integrations that contributed to a higher volume of sales rather than to the aftermarket segment. This shift is accompanied with recuring revenues.
  • Cost of sales increased by $397,251, or 83.3%, to $873,861 for the year ended December 31, 2024, compared to $476,610 for the year ended December 31, 2023. The increase was mainly due to the increase in the volume of sales during the year ended December 31, 2024 and an inventory write off of approximately $114,680
  • Research and development expenses increased by $1,506,552, or 237%, to $2,143,353 for the year ended December 31, 2024, compared to $636,801 for the year ended December 31, 2023. The increase resulted mainly from labor costs of $843,881 accompanied an increase in by materials and experiments costs of $386,839.
  • Sales and marketing expenses increased by $593,099, or 121.6%, to $1,081,003 for the year ended December 31, 2024, compared to $487,904 for the year ended December 31, 2023. The increase resulted mainly from labor, professional services and more subcontractors’ costs of $449,788 accompanied an increase in by travel and conferences participation costs of $122,288.
  • General and administrative expenses increased by $927,128, or 62.9%, to $2,400,000 for the year ended December 31, 2024, compared to $1,472,872 for the year ended December 31, 2023. The increase resulted mainly from professional services rendered after the IPO and costs associated with becoming a public company.
  • Other finance income, net was $200,724 for the year ended December 31, 2024, compared to finance income, net of $210,675 for the year ended December 31, 2023. Other finance income, net, primarily includes income from interest on deposits and exchange rate differences.
  • Net loss and comprehensive loss increased by $7,282,851, or 193%, to $11,054,230 for the year ended December 31, 2024, compared to a net loss of $3,771,379 for the year ended December 31, 2023. The increase was the result of an increase of mainly a non-cash item, changes in fair value of derivative warrant liabilities, along with an increase in operating expenses, as described above.
  • The loss per share in 2024, was $0.99 compared to net loss per share of $0.77 in 2023.
  • As of December 31, 2024, the company’s cash and cash equivalents was approximately $4.2 million and the company’s total shareholders’ deficit was approximately $0.3 million.

As a result of the warrant exercise and registered direct offering in January 2025 and February 2025, and as of the date of this current report, the Company believes it has stockholders’ equity of $3.6 million.

A copy of ParaZero’s annual report on Form 20-F for the year ended December 31, 2024 has been filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission at https://www.sec.gov/ and posted on ParaZero’s investor relations website at https://parazero.com/investor-relations/. ParaZero will deliver a hard copy of its annual report, including its complete audited consolidated financial statements, free of charge, to its shareholders upon request at michal@efraty.com.

About ParaZero Technologies

ParaZero is an aerospace company focused on drone safety systems for defense and commercial drones and urban air mobility aircraft. Started in 2014 by a passionate group of aviation professionals and drone industry veterans, ParaZero designs smart, autonomous parachute safety systems designed to enable safe flight operations over populated areas and beyond-visual-line-of-sight (BVLOS) as well as for various military applications including Counter UAS. For more information about ParaZero, please visit https://parazero.com/

AUTOZONE INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
AutoZone Inc., a leading retailer and distributor of automotive replacement parts and accessories, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of fiscal 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, providing insights into its growth strategies and market dynamics.

Financial Highlights

AutoZone Inc. reported the following key financial metrics for the twelve weeks ended February 15, 2025:

  • Net Sales: $3,952 million, an increase of 2.4% over the comparable prior year period, driven by a 2.9% increase in total company same store sales on a constant currency basis.
  • Gross Profit: $2,128 million, consistent with the prior year period, with a gross margin of 53.9% benefiting from higher merchandise margins.
  • Operating Profit: $706.8 million, a decrease of 4.9% due to unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates and non-cash LIFO favorability in the prior year quarter.
  • Net Income: $487.9 million, a decrease of 5.3% from the comparable prior year period, impacted by foreign currency exchange rates and LIFO adjustments.
  • Diluted EPS: $28.29, a decrease of 2.1% from the prior year period, with a $0.06 per share increase from stock repurchases since the end of the comparable prior year period.

Business Highlights

AutoZone Inc. also reported significant operational achievements and strategic initiatives during the period:

  • Revenue Segments: The Auto Parts Stores segment, which includes domestic and international operations, reported net sales of $3.87 billion for the twelve weeks ended February 15, 2025. The Other category, which includes ALLDATA and E-commerce, contributed $77.6 million in net sales for the same period.
  • Geographical Performance: Domestic same store sales increased by 1.9% on a constant currency basis, while international same store sales decreased by 8.2% but showed a 9.5% increase on a constant currency basis. The total company same store sales increased by 2.9% on a constant currency basis.
  • Sales Units: During the twenty-four weeks ended February 15, 2025, AutoZone opened 79 net new stores, compared to 51 net new stores in the comparable prior year period. This expansion includes both domestic and international locations.
  • New Production Launches: The company opened two new domestic distribution centers, which contributed to increased supply chain costs but are part of the strategic growth initiatives.
  • Future Outlook: AutoZone plans to continue its growth initiatives, including new store openings and hub and mega hub store expansion projects. The company expects to increase its investment in these areas compared to fiscal 2024, leveraging internally generated funds and available borrowing capacity.

SEC Filing: AUTOZONE INC [ AZO ] - 10-Q - Mar. 21, 2025
